Phase 1: Spatial Architecture & Layout M.Trigger Context: Establishing padding, margins, and grid alignments.
Execution Logic: Enforce an absolute 8-point base unit system (e.g., 4, 8, 16, 24, 32, 48, 64, 96px).
Apply internal padding (8-16px), related element gaps (8-16px), unrelated gaps (24-32px), and section breaks (48-96px).
Never use arbitrary prime numbers.
Apply the Rule of Thirds and Fibonacci sequence constraints for macro-layout balance.

Phase 1: Spatial Architecture & Layout M.Trigger Context: Generating dashboards or feature-rich landing pages.
Execution Logic: Inventory all content first.
Construct a strict 12-column (desktop) or 4-column (mobile) grid.
Partition data into modular 2x2 bento containers using CSS Grid (minmax, auto-fit).
Map visual flow using F-Pattern or Z-Pattern heuristics, placing the highest-priority metrics in the top-left.

Phase 1: Spatial Architecture & Layout M.Trigger Context: Designing reusable UI components for multiple viewports.
Execution Logic: Reject viewport-based @media queries for internal components.
Use container-type: inline-size.
Define 3 variants minimum: Compact (max 300px), Medium (max 600px), and Expanded (min 601px).
Scale gracefully between them.

Phase 1: Spatial Architecture & Layout M.Trigger Context: Generating interactive buttons or data chips.
Execution Logic: Calculate the text's computed line height + vertical paddings.
Enforce the mathematical ratio: Target Width = Computed Height × 2.
Overrule this ratio only to guarantee minimum WCAG mobile touch-target sizes of 44x44px.
Limit to 2 font families (e.g., Sans-serif for UI, Serif for Display).
Establish a base size (16px desktop, 14px mobile).
Scale headings using a strict geometric ratio (e.g., 1.25x or 1.5x) via the formula size_n = base_size × ratio^(n-1).
Limit line lengths to 60-75 characters to prevent reading fatigue.
Assign line heights programmatically: Body text (1.5x font size), Small text (1.4x), Headings (1.1x to 1.2x).
Apply a kerning (letter-spacing) reduction of -2% to -3% exclusively on large display text to tighten visual presence.

Phase 2: Mathematical Typography & Icono.Trigger Context: Placing vector icons adjacent to text elements.
Execution Logic: Extract the adjacent text's computed CSS line height (not font size).
Force the SVG bounding box to match this exact pixel integer (e.g., 24px box for a 16px font with a 1.5x line height).
Apply align-items: center to eliminate sub-pixel baseline misalignment.
Implement a 4-layer functional system: Layer 1 (Background canvas), Layer 2 (Elevated Surfaces), Layer 3 (Text/Icons), Layer 4 (Accents/CTAs).
Utilize triadic color schemes for harmony.
Ensure text opacity cascades (87% primary, 60% secondary, 38% tertiary).
Aggressively nullify all box-shadow properties.
Calculate Z-axis elevation via HSL lightness (L).
Set the base canvas ≤ 15% lightness. For every Z-index step upward, increase lightness by 3% to 5%.
Automatically desaturate accent colors by 15-20% and invert text inside chips to prevent retina halation.
Render shadows virtually invisible to the naked eye.
Maximize blur radius and drastically reduce the opacity channel.
Reserve pronounced shadows strictly for transient, high-elevation elements (Dropdowns, Popovers) that break the primary layout grid.

Phase 3: Color Systems & Dimensional Phy.Trigger Context: Designing overlays, modals, or premium SaaS cards.
Execution Logic: Apply background blur (10-20px) coupled with a 1px low-opacity white inside border and a subtle inner shadow to simulate glass volume.
Limit backdrop-filter usage to less than 50% of the viewport to prevent severe rendering performance degradation.

Phase 4: Component States & Interaction.Trigger Context: Defining the interactive states of any UI primitive.
Execution Logic: Generate strict visual variables for 6+ states: Default, Hover, Active/Pressed, Focus, Disabled, Loading, Error, Success.
Default (baseline), Hover (1.02x scale, +5% brightness, expanded shadow)
Active/Pressed (0.98x scale, inset shadow), Focus (2px semantic ring)
Disabled (0.5 opacity, grayscale background), Loading (continuous shimmer mask)
Error (semantic red tint), Success

Phase 4: Component States & Interaction.Trigger Context: Determining the UI paradigm for a triggered user task.
Execution Logic: Run Hick's Law assessment. If the task is simple (≤ 2 inputs), non-destructive, and requires visual context of the main dashboard, generate a non-blocking Popover.
If the task is complex, destructive, or initiates a new workflow, generate a full-screen, background-blurred Modal to force cognitive focus.
Bind click event. Intercept network delay by instantly mutating the local DOM (e.g., removing a deleted row visually) and firing a success Toast.
Process the database fetch asynchronously. Revert the DOM state and fire an Error Toast only if the server returns a non-200 status code.
Enforce strict timing bounds: 150-200ms for micro-interactions, 200-300ms for component transitions.
Assign ease-out for entering elements and ease-in for exiting elements.
Apply a strict 300ms delay constraint to hover-based tooltips to prevent accidental rapid-fire triggering.
Map interactions natively (<button> for actions, <a> for navigation).
Verify keyboard tab index logic.
Enforce a minimum 4.5:1 text contrast and 3:1 component boundary contrast.
Inject @media (prefers-reduced-motion) constraints that nullify transition durations down to 0.01ms.
Scan CSS architecture. Strip transitions attached to layout properties (width, height, margin, padding).
Permit animations exclusively on hardware-accelerated properties (transform and opacity).
Identify and purge Unicode emojis acting as system icons, replacing them with Phosphor/Lucide vectors.
Scan for and mathematically mute hyper-saturated hex codes.
Detect 'dead cards' lacking actionable data and dynamically collapse/remove them from the DOM.
Strip 'lorem ipsum' and inject realistic, context-aware placeholder data.

Phase 6: System Architecture & Presentat.Trigger Context: Structuring a finalized design system for developer handoff.
Execution Logic: Build from the token layer up. Define CSS/Design tokens as the single source of truth.
Define the Component API strictly (Props, Defaults, Constraints, Slots).
Map out progressive disclosure empty states (clear explanation + resolution action) for every data container.

Phase 6: System Architecture & Presentat.Trigger Context: Exporting designs for portfolio or stakeholder review.
Execution Logic: For Creative/Portfolio Mode: Apply WebGL/CSS 3D skews (e.g., transform: perspective(1000px) rotateX(2deg) rotateY(-14deg)), offsetting sub-components along the Z-axis with high-blur drop shadows to create an exploded collage.

For Professional/Enterprise Mode: Integrate the flat UI specifically into realistic, AI-generated environmental lifestyle mockups (e.g., Midjourney natural desk lighting) to synthesize market-ready tangibility.
